authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 422.111 - Disclosure requirements
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal Medicare Advantage disclosure regulation. It directly supports provider network and provider directory information controls that constrain publication readiness.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: directory publication intake and disclosure readiness
used_for: member-facing disclosure and audit evidence expectations

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 422.116 - Network adequacy
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal Medicare Advantage network adequacy regulation. It identifies provider and facility-specialty types, HSD reference files, provider supply evidence, and submission support expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: network metadata and access-impact dependency review
used_for: access-impact escalation when provider changes affect service area coverage

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 438.214 - Provider selection
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary Medicaid managed care regulation for provider selection policies and credentialing or recredentialing standards. It supports onboarding and credentialing-dependency refusal boundaries.

authoritative_sources.title: CMS Interoperability and Prior Authorization Final Rule - Provider Directory API
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS public interoperability guidance is authoritative payer/provider operating context for provider-directory API requirements and field-level directory information exchange.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: directory API payload and publication evidence handling

authoritative_sources.title: National Provider Identifier Standard (NPI)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS administers the HIPAA NPI standard. NPI is the unique provider identifier used across administrative and financial transactions and is required for safe roster and directory reconciliation.
used_for: claims, referral, and provider-network system crosswalks

authoritative_sources.title: Provider requirements and resources - No Surprises rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS No Surprises resources identify provider directory accuracy and continuity-of-care protections, making unsupported provider status changes and inaccurate directory publication operationally unsafe.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: directory accuracy and continuity-of-care risk review
used_for: continuity and directory accuracy risk framing

authoritative_sources.title: HIPAA Minimum Necessary Standard
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR is the HIPAA privacy regulator. The minimum-necessary guidance constrains provider issue tickets that include member PHI, claim details, referral facts, or appointment history.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: PHI-sensitive issue handling and disclosure gating

authoritative_sources.title: Health Plan Accreditation public standards context for provider network management
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NCQA is a recognized health plan accreditation body. Its public health plan accreditation context supports provider network management, credentialing, recredentialing, delegation, and quality workflow terminology.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: provider network management and credentialing workflow normalization
used_for: provider network lifecycle and exception handling

workflow_questions.question: What prerequisites or authorizations gate the workflow?
workflow_questions.answer: Provider-network actions require confirmed provider entity, NPI, TIN, product/network, payer, plan, provider type, service location, specialty or facility type, service area, requested action, effective date, credentialing and recredentialing status, contract status, roster source, directory publication target, tenant source hierarchy, approval matrix, publication cadence, privacy posture, and rollback criteria. Onboarding and active publication are gated by credentialing and contract evidence; termination publication is gated by contract approval, effective date, notice or access-impact review, and directory suppression evidence.

workflow_questions.question: Which records or systems establish the patient, member, or claim truth set?
workflow_questions.answer: The provider truth set is established by provider network management, credentialing, contract lifecycle, roster feed, directory publishing, NPI/NPPES reference, CRM, case management, document repository, and audit systems. When provider issues affect a member, claim, referral, or authorization, the member or claim truth set comes from eligibility, referral, authorization, claims, payer portal, care management, privacy/consent, and audit systems; this lane can package those dependencies but cannot decide coverage, authorization, or claim outcomes.

workflow_questions.question: Where does the lane stop and licensed clinical, privacy, or payer-interpretation authority begin?
workflow_questions.answer: The lane stops before credentialing committee signoff, contract approval, payer policy interpretation, network adequacy legal interpretation, privacy release decisions, medical necessity, diagnosis, treatment planning, clinical appropriateness, clinical capability determination, coding, claims payment, or appeal handling. Those require adjacent owner escalation or orchestrator return.

workflow_stages.name: Provider-network intake and boundary classification
workflow_stages.description: Capture provider entity, identifiers, product/network, location, requested action, issue class, affected member or claim sensitivity, and authority context before treating the request as actionable.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Provider identity and source-system reconciliation
workflow_stages.description: Reconcile NPI, TIN, service location, specialty, credentialing status, contract metadata, roster delta, directory record, CRM ticket, and case-management task under the tenant source-of-truth hierarchy.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Lifecycle state progression and dependency validation
workflow_stages.description: Move onboarding, maintenance, directory correction, termination, or issue-management work through accepted, validated, blocked, owner-routed, approved-to-publish, published, suppressed, complete, hold, rollback, and rework states only when supporting evidence exists.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Exception queue and adjacent-owner routing
workflow_stages.description: Route missing credentialing approval, missing contract approval, unsupported directory exception, eligibility/referral/authorization dependency, source conflict, PHI-sensitive handling, access-impact risk, or payer/clinical authority issue to the accountable owner.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Directory publication, suppression, and completion evidence
workflow_stages.description: Record changed fields, publication target, publication batch, suppression proof, owner acknowledgement, rollback condition, residual risk, and audit trail before claiming completion.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

artifacts: Provider roster files and delegated roster feeds
Provider entity records, NPI, TIN, service location, specialty, facility type, and product/network mappings
Contract metadata, provider agreement status, effective dates, termination dates, and amendment pointers
Provider directory records, provider-directory API payloads, publication queues, and publication batch logs
Health Service Delivery files, provider supply references, network adequacy support files, and service-area metadata
CRM tickets, provider relations notes, case-management tasks, and issue logs
Eligibility, referral, authorization, claims, member-access, and payer portal records when provider issues are member-specific
Consent, privacy, minimum-necessary, access-control, audit, and retention records
Roster or directory correction summaries, exception queue notes, and completion evidence records
systems_of_record: Directory publishing system and provider-directory API
CRM and provider relations case system
Claims, referral, authorization, eligibility, and payer portal systems for member-specific dependencies
Document repository and delegated entity exchange folders
Privacy, consent, identity, access, audit, and retention systems

allowed: Determine whether a provider issue is roster, credentialing, contract, directory, access, payer-operations, privacy-sensitive, or adjacent-owner related based on retrieved evidence.
Advance, hold, rework, or complete provider-network lifecycle states when source-system, approval, publication, and audit evidence supports the transition.
Prepare provider-issue disposition notes, roster or directory correction summaries, exception queue notes, and completion evidence records.

Route provider lifecycle work to credentialing, contracting, payer operations, provider relations, compliance, privacy, patient access, referral, authorization, revenue cycle, clinical review, or legal owners.
escalate: Credentialing approval, recredentialing exceptions, delegated credentialing oversight, or clinical competence questions require credentialing or clinical owner escalation.
Contract approval, reimbursement term interpretation, provider agreement disputes, termination authority, or payer contract conflicts require contracting, legal, or compliance escalation.

Member eligibility, referral, authorization, coverage, medical necessity, payer policy, claim, or appeal questions require patient access, utilization management, payer operations, revenue cycle, or appeals owners.
PHI disclosure uncertainty, broad distribution of member-specific issue details, consent ambiguity, or minimum-necessary conflict requires privacy or compliance escalation.
Patient safety, access-to-care risk, clinical appropriateness, sparse network clinical implications, or urgent symptoms require clinical or patient access escalation.
refuse: Refuse to publish unsupported active, in-network, accepting-new-patients, specialty, location, panel, termination, or product participation status.

Refuse to approve contracts, credentialing committee outcomes, network adequacy legal sufficiency, payer coverage, authorization, medical necessity, coding, claim payment, appeal outcomes, or privacy releases.
Refuse to close provider-network work when source-system update, publication proof, owner acknowledgement, rollback condition, or audit evidence is missing or contradictory.

domain_failure_modes: Incorrect provider status or location causes a member to seek care from an unavailable or out-of-network provider.
Publishing a provider before credentialing or contract approval creates compliance, claims, access, and member harm risk.
Suppressing or terminating a provider without effective date and access-impact evidence disrupts continuity of care.
Roster, credentialing, contract, and directory systems disagree and the lane chooses a winner without tenant source hierarchy.
Member-specific provider complaint details are broadly shared, exposing PHI beyond minimum-necessary handling.
Provider network operations decides coverage, authorization, clinical appropriateness, or payer policy from administrative provider data.
Directory publication completes without audit trail, publication proof, or rollback criteria.
State Medicaid, CMS, payer contract, and tenant policy conflict is hidden instead of escalated.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing authorization, referral, or eligibility dependency
PHI or privacy-sensitive request that requires controlled handling
Boundary case that would require licensed clinical or payer-interpretation escalation
Roster, credentialing, and directory data disagree across systems.
A provider onboarding or termination step is blocked on missing approvals.
A request seeks to publish unsupported provider status changes.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State-specific Medicaid, DOI, and payer contract timing for directory updates
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: No blocking ambiguity remains for the public validated baseline. Runtime must retrieve jurisdiction, payer, contract, and tenant publication policy before final actions.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant source-of-truth hierarchy across delegated rosters and internal provider systems
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The spec requires escalation when source hierarchy is missing or systems conflict; tenant configuration supplies the operational hierarchy.

notes: The source set covers public healthcare regulator guidance, public payer/provider operating guidance, and recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance.

The final pack constrains provider network operations to administrative lifecycle execution and evidence handling, with explicit credentialing, contracting, payer, privacy, clinical, compliance, and legal boundaries.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated specification-based package.
